body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: JSON Meta Application Protocol (JMAP) for Calendars
JMAP extension for S/MIME signing and encryption
Use of VAPID in JMAP WebPush

push system in jmap at the moment but you can only opt to receive a push when any message is delivered or when there's any change at all in email and on uh iOS in particular but possibly Android too uh they really only want you to wake up the device when you want to show the user a notification which may only be a subset of those and apps can get penalized if they don't start showing notifications whenever they get a push so uh one of the key things this is meant to add if we go to the next slide I think I put this in is that we'll only send push for messages where the device will show notification and the way that will work is that when you uh register for pushes you'll be able to pass the server um a filter the same format we use for email query and just say only send a push if it matches this filter um and so uh because of the way the jmap filter uh works you can be pretty much as arbitrarily precise as you want for that uh so that should be quite nice uh and then the second thing is not quite as important but still a nice optimization in that it's nice when you receive that uh not to have to make another HTTP request in order to show the notification to the user uh especially because push notifications can get through sometimes when uh you still have pretty crappy um connections and that thing May Fail and so the second thing you'd be able to do with this if
